LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS 699 SECOND DEVELOPMENT, LLC AND CANNON CONSTRUCTORS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PERMIT PLAINTIFF AN OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY FACTS THAT SHOW HOW, WHEN, WHERE, TO WHOM, AND BY WHAT MEANS THE REPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE. (LAZAR V. SUP. CT. (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, 645.) ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE SPECIFIC FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANTS' HAD NO REASONABLE GROUND FOR BELIEVING THE REPRESENTATION TO BE TRUE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047 (302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JESSICA M. RYLAND OF THE HAIGHT LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 546-7500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS 699 SECOND DEVELOPMENT, LLC and CANNON CONSTRUCTORS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendants' Demurrer to Plaintiff's eighth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is sustained with ten days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff an opportunity to allege with the requisite specificity facts that show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were made. (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Additionally, Plaintiff must allege specific facts establishing that Defendants' had no reasonable ground for believing the representation to be true. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185, Tel. No. (951)764-4047 =302/MJM

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-22-2013 Set For Trial On Sep-15-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion Seeking Leave File And Serve Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-15-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: THOMAS PURTELL, ESQ., 415.437.1800, FOR PLAINTIFF 88 TOWNSEND STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION.
JESSICA LASSERE RYLAND, ESQ., 415.546.7500, FOR DEFENDANTS 699 SECOND DEVELOPMENT LLC AND CANNON CONSTRUCTORS, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: PLAINTIFF 88 TOWNSEND STREET OWNERS ASOCIATION's Motion Seeking Leave To File And Serve Second Amended Complaint is granted. Plaintiff may file its proposed Second Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. There is an established policy that "trial courts are to liberally permit'amendments, at any stage of the proceeding." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.) Defendants have not established that Plaintiff was unreasonably dilatory in seeking the amendment or that the amendment would be unduly prejudicial. Defendants' request for a trial continuance is GRANTED; the current trial date of September 15, 2014 is continued to November 10, 2014 at 9:30 am in Department 206. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-15-2014 Text Ruling

JESSICA LASSERE RYLAND, ESQ., 415.546.7500, FOR DEFENDANTS 699 SECOND DEVELOPMENT LLC AND CANNON CONSTRUCTORS, INC.

RULING: No argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS 699 SECOND DEVELOPMENT, LLC and CANNON CONSTRUCTORS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied as moot, in light of the ruling on Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a Second Amended Complaint. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
